question: Company A sells a board game. According to the chart, which of the following statements is true?

options.A: The latest year has clearly experienced a permanent collapse in demand.
options.B: The latest year shows a sharp drop in copies sold compared to previous years.
options.C: The latest year has the lowest full-year copies sold in the entire period.
options.D: The chart only shows sales up to an early point in the latest year, so it does not justify conclusions about the full-year trend.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 5 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (640 copies) directly to full-year values like 1,533 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
